reasoning: The 'unsafe' verdict was based on a contributor script (show-download-count.sh) not executed during build/install. AUR safety scope excludes development tooling unless referenced in PKGBUILD/.install scripts.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged a non-build script (show-download-count.sh) as unsafe due to potential command injection, while Deepseek correctly focused on build-relevant files.
coverage_issues: Qwen prioritized contrib scripts over core build files like meson.build
false_positives: Qwen's show-download-count.sh verdict: Contributor script not executed during makepkg
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Weight PKGBUILD references higher than contrib/ scripts
Enhance context awareness: Train models to distinguish build-time vs development scripts
Reduce false positives: Explicitly exclude non-build scripts in assessment criteria
